ordinaryShadcnBaseline.summary: Ordinary Shadcn Calendar provides a styled calendar selection surface; consumer-specific unavailable-date reason association and native alternate form entry remain composition work.

mergoraAdvantage.summary: Mergora combines an RTL-aware localized keyboard grid with a real native date input, optional per-date recovery explanations, and a bounded range-highlight/preview composition seam.

visualSignature.summary: Literal Canvas surfaces, precise Ink boundaries, strong temporal hierarchy, restrained Green/Violet signals, the shared contrast-backed focus seam, logical layout, and control radii keep this system recognizably Mergora.

optionalEnhancements.summary: Consumer-supplied reasons are associated with unavailable date buttons only when requested.

api.enableWhen: Set showAvailabilityExplanations to its enabled value.

disabledBehavior.ui: No enhancement-specific node or visual surface is rendered.
disabledBehavior.behavior: The native/basic temporal selection and form path remains authoritative with no enhancement calculation or state.
disabledBehavior.events: No enhancement-specific callback can fire because its trigger is absent.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: No enhancement-specific group, description, relationship, live output, or focus target is exposed.

optionalEnhancements.summary: Range systems can opt into semantic start/end/within attributes and pointer or keyboard preview callbacks without changing Calendar's value model.

api.enableWhen: Supply highlightRange and, when interactive preview is needed, onDatePreviewChange.

disabledBehavior.ui: No range start, end, or within attributes are rendered.
disabledBehavior.behavior: Calendar retains its standalone selected-date model with no range-preview state.
disabledBehavior.events: No preview pointer, blur, or callback behavior is attached.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: No preview output or relationship is added to the standalone calendar.

accessibilityEvidence.summary: Native semantics, labels, keyboard models, an emulated coarse-pointer context with 44 CSS-pixel day geometry, preferred navigation heights, and a real tap, narrow reflow, RTL, forced-colors, reduced-motion, and automated axe targets are present; current manual desktop/mobile screen-reader, speech, switch, and real-device evidence is missing.

interactionEvidence.summary: Focused unit and browser targets cover canonical values, clean enhancement removal, keyboard/pointer selection, native form lifecycle, responsive layout, RTL, and preference modes; the full manager/framework/engine matrix has not yet run.

parityEvidence.summary: Deterministic generation derives every package implementation byte from normalized canonical source and matches native and Shadcn payloads to the same consumer transform; the recorded exact-tarball Next.js and Vite package/source matrix passes.

blockers.summary: Current desktop/mobile screen-reader, speech, switch, and applicable real-device evidence is not recorded.

maturityAssessment.rationale: Deterministic delivery parity is verified, but the remaining interaction, lifecycle, browser, accessibility, scale, security, or release evidence recorded for this item is still insufficient for promotion.

ordinaryShadcnBaseline.summary: Ordinary Shadcn Input can use datetime-local, but explicit unzoned semantics and independently associated timezone interpretation remain consumer documentation.

mergoraAdvantage.summary: Mergora preserves the native unzoned local wall-time value while optionally making timezone interpretation explicit and resolving repeated or nonexistent wall times through a consumer-supplied adapter and explicit ambiguity policy; deterministic UTC, New York, Berlin, and Kolkata boundary fixtures prove the seam without claiming time-zone database ownership.

visualSignature.summary: Literal Canvas surfaces, precise Ink boundaries, strong temporal hierarchy, restrained Green/Violet signals, the shared contrast-backed focus seam, logical layout, and control radii keep this system recognizably Mergora.

optionalEnhancements.summary: An IANA zone label or consumer-rendered interpretation is associated without converting the value.

api.enableWhen: Set showTimeZoneContext to its enabled value.

disabledBehavior.ui: No enhancement-specific node or visual surface is rendered.
disabledBehavior.behavior: The native/basic temporal selection and form path remains authoritative with no enhancement calculation or state.
disabledBehavior.events: No enhancement-specific callback can fire because its trigger is absent.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: No enhancement-specific group, description, relationship, live output, or focus target is exposed.

optionalEnhancements.summary: A synchronous consumer adapter classifies valid, repeated, and nonexistent local times; reject is the safe default while earlier/later selection, recoverable native validity, optional resolved-instant form data, and deterministic server/client boundary hydration remain explicit. The adapter remains consumer-owned; Mergora does not bundle a time-zone database.

api.enableWhen: Supply wallTimeAdapter and an explicit timeZone.

disabledBehavior.ui: No wall-time status or resolved hidden input is rendered.
disabledBehavior.behavior: The adapter is not called; the native datetime-local value remains unzoned and no custom validity is added.
disabledBehavior.events: onWallTimeResolutionChange does not fire.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: No wall-time live output, invalid state, alert relationship, or hidden form control is exposed.

accessibilityEvidence.summary: Native semantics, labels, keyboard models, an emulated coarse-pointer context with 44 CSS-pixel day geometry, preferred navigation heights, and a real tap, narrow reflow, RTL, forced-colors, reduced-motion, wall-time recovery, and automated axe targets are present; current manual desktop/mobile screen-reader, speech, switch, and real-device evidence is missing.

interactionEvidence.summary: Focused unit and browser targets cover canonical values, clean enhancement removal, keyboard/pointer selection, native form lifecycle, deterministic UTC/New York/Berlin/Kolkata wall-time boundary hydration, responsive layout, RTL, and preference modes; a direct Storybook specimen also proves controlled ownership with timezone context and wall-time resolution disabled. The full manager/framework/engine matrix has not yet run.

parityEvidence.summary: Deterministic generation derives every package implementation byte from normalized canonical source and matches native and Shadcn payloads to the same consumer transform; the recorded exact-tarball Next.js and Vite package/source matrix passes.
